title: ErythroPOietin Alfa to Prevent Mortality and Reduce Severe Disability in Critically Ill TRAUMA Patients

skipped_criteria: < 24 hours since primary traumatic injury (temporal criterion relative to injury time; no injury_time field in schema).
Invasively mechanically ventilated (no ventilation_status field in schema).
Expected to stay in ICU ≥ 48 hours (no predicted_icu_los field in schema).
Haemoglobin not exceeding ULN reference range (no hemoglobin field in schema).
Informed consent from legal surrogate (consent/legal process criterion; not a patient data field).
GCS = 3 exclusion is encoded, but the additional qualifier 'fixed dilated pupils' cannot be expressed (no pupil_response field in schema).
Recent history of DVT, PE or other thromboembolic event within previous 12 months (no thromboembolic_history field; anticoagulant_use partially covers concomitant anticoagulant treatment).
Chronic hypercoagulable disorder including known malignancy (no coagulation_disorder or malignancy field in schema).
Treatment with EPO in the last 30 days (no prior_epo_treatment field in schema).
First dose of study drug unable to be given within 24 hours of primary injury (operational/logistical criterion; no field in schema).
Lactation or 3 months postpartum (no lactation or postpartum field in schema).
Expected to die imminently (< 24 hours) (no imminent_death_expected field in schema).
Known sensitivity to mammalian cell derived products (no allergy/sensitivity field in schema).
Known contraindication to epoetin alfa (no drug-specific contraindication field in schema).
End stage renal failure receiving chronic dialysis (no renal_failure or dialysis field in schema).
Severe pre-existing physical or mental disability or severe co-morbidity (no disability or comorbidity field in schema).
Treating physician believes randomisation not in patient's best interest (clinical judgment criterion; not encodable in schema).
